description: Ketubba fragment. Dating: Perhaps 10th or 11th century (Goitein just writes "old.") Vertical strip from the middle. The dowry list mentions a bed of shisham/sheesham wood (sarīr sāsim/sāsam), 6 dyed pillows, and an item called עקביה כחלי. The order: first bedding, then clothing. There are five signatures partially preserved. (Information from Goitein’s index card)
